gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: tph-1 in the ADF neuron; resolved tph-1 to WB:WBGene00006600.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: ser-6 in the AWB neuron; resolved ser-6 to WB:WBGene00021897.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: mod-1 in the URX neuron; resolved mod-1 to WB:WBGene00003386.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: tbh-1 in the RIC neuron; resolved tbh-1 to WB:WBGene00006541.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: nhr-76 in the INTESTINE; resolved nhr-76 to WB:WBGene00015497.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/atgl1/enabled_by.snippet: Figure Panel E: the lipase labelled 'ATGL' in the INTESTINE corresponds to C. elegans atgl-1; resolved to WB:WBGene00015484. Single node for both the 'ATGL' and 'atgl-1' figure mentions.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/molecular_f.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (rate-limiting serotonin biosynthesis enzyme), consistent with its role in ADF in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/molecular_f.snippet: ser-6 is annotated (IDA) to octopamine receptor activity; in Panel E ser-6 in AWB receives octopamine (Oct) from RIC.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/molecular_f.snippet: mod-1 is annotated (IDA) to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity; in Panel E mod-1 in URX is the direct receptor for 5-HT from ADF.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/molecular_f.snippet: tbh-1 is annotated (IMP) to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity; produces octopamine in RIC in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/molecular_.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to nuclear receptor activity (ligand-dependent DNA-binding TF); in Panel E nhr-76 in the intestine induces ATGL/atgl-1.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/atgl1/molecular_.snippet: atgl-1 (figure label 'ATGL') is annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity; the intestinal lipase driving lipolysis in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 functions in serotonin (5-HT) biosynthesis in ADF, the upstream signal of the circuit in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 mediates octopamine signaling; in Panel E it transduces the octopamine (Oct) signal from RIC in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 transduces the serotonin (5-HT) signal in URX in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 synthesizes octopamine in RIC, the signal acting on AWB/ser-6 in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 positively regulates transcription (of the lipase atgl-1) in the intestine; annotation derives from the same paper as this figure (Noble et al. 2013).
gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure1-007/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 (ATGL) lipase drives triglyceride catabolism/lipolysis; captures the figure's 'ATGL --> beta-oxidation / Fat loss' arrow (the downstream process) as the BP this activity is part_of.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Panel E: tph-1 labelled above the blue triangle 'ADF' (an amphid sensory neuron) inside the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/occurs_in.snippet: tph-1 localizes to the neuron projection.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/occurs_in/c.snippet: Panel E: ser-6 labelled above the green triangle 'AWB' (an amphid sensory neuron) inside the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/occurs_in.snippet: ser-6 (GPCR) localizes to the plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Panel E: mod-1 labelled above the yellow hexagon 'URX' (oxygen-sensing sensory neuron) inside the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/occurs_in.snippet: mod-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel localizes to the plasma membrane (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Panel E: tbh-1 labelled above the red pentagon 'RIC' (an octopaminergic interneuron) inside the NEURONS compartment.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-76 (nuclear receptor / DNA-binding TF) localizes to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/atgl1/occurs_in.snippet: atgl-1 localizes to the lipid droplet (IDA), where it hydrolyzes stored triglyceride in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/has_output/.snippet: tph-1 in ADF produces serotonin (5-HT), the signal shown on the ADF->URX arrow ('5-HT') in Panel E. Producing end of the 5-HT relay to mod-1.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/has_small_m.snippet: Serotonin directly gates the mod-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel (RO:0012001); 5-HT from ADF activates mod-1 in URX in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/has_output/.snippet: tbh-1 (tyramine beta-hydroxylase) in RIC produces octopamine, the signal labelled 'Oct' on the RIC->AWB arrow in Panel E. Producing end of the octopamine relay to ser-6.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/has_small_m.snippet: Octopamine directly binds/activates the ser-6 octopamine receptor (RO:0012001); Oct from RIC activates ser-6 in AWB in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/has_input/.snippet: nhr-76 (intestinal nuclear receptor) transcriptionally induces the lipase atgl-1 (ATGL); atgl-1 is the regulated target gene (has_input) of nhr-76's transcriptional activity in Panel E.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tph1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E solid arrow ADF->URX labelled '5-HT': tph-1 product serotonin directly positively regulates mod-1. Serotonin made by tph-1 (ADF) directly gates the mod-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel (URX) — production of a small-molecule regulator (shared-ChEBI relay 5-HT).

gomodel:run-figure1-007/tbh1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E solid arrow RIC->AWB labelled 'Oct': tbh-1 product octopamine directly positively regulates ser-6. Octopamine made by tbh-1 (RIC) directly binds the ser-6 octopamine receptor (AWB) — production of a small-molecule regulator (shared-ChEBI relay octopamine).

gomodel:run-figure1-007/mod1/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E dashed arrow URX->RIC: mod-1 activity in URX indirectly positively regulates tbh-1 (octopamine synthesis) in RIC. Dashed line indicates an indirect/multi-step relay.

Panel E dashed arrow URX->INTESTINE labelled 'endocrine signal' (granules drawn near URX): the URX neuron (mod-1 activity) releases an as-yet-unidentified endocrine signal that reaches the intestine and indirectly positively regulates the intestinal nuclear receptor nhr-76. Compartment-level NEURONS->INTESTINE edge modeled between the most plausible upstream activity (mod-1/URX) and downstream activity (nhr-76/intestine); the signal's molecular identity is out of scope.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/nhr76/causal/gom.snippet: Panel E solid arrow nhr-76->ATGL: nhr-76 (intestinal nuclear receptor) indirectly positively regulates atgl-1/ATGL by inducing its transcription (gene expression is the intervening module); TF->target indirect relation.

gomodel:run-figure1-007/ser6/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E solid arrow AWB->ADF: ser-6 (AWB) appears to act positively upstream of tph-1 (ADF). LOW CONFIDENCE — this arrow was flagged as unconfirmed in the vision verification pass (conf ~0.3); mechanism unknown, so modeled as causally upstream of, positive effect. Curator should verify direction/existence against the figure.
